engine.ladder_reasoning: A filmmaker/actor: the films, roles, festival selections and critical praise ARE the proof; the visitor evaluates the body of work, not a defined service package to hire or a product to buy.

goal.one_action: subscribe to the newsletter / follow to keep up with his films (secondary: pre-order the film, donate)

structure.archetype_note: Personal creative-brand hub: bio/CV credibility spine + per-film subpages + newsletter + social/linktree/donate outlets. Not a single-action business site; conversion is diffuse.

mapped.observed_as: Adrian Țofei • Official Site – Filmmaker & Actor
CV: Film Credits / Festivals, Awards & Nominations / Education
Subscribe to receive the latest news / Contact Adrian

unmapped.observed: Share this: (Facebook/X/Reddit/WhatsApp/Bluesky… share buttons on every page)
unmapped.why_no_fit: followStrip is about following the brand's own accounts; this is an outbound content-SHARING widget (visitor amplifies the page), a distinct engagement affordance
no canonical timeline section id; it is a chronological credit ledger, richer than results/testimonials

proposed_new.name: works-catalog
proposed_new.evidence: Movies nav opens per-film subpages (Be My Cat, We Put the World to Sleep) presenting the creator's OWN films — poster, trailer, awards, pre-order link — as the site spine, matching the registered works-catalog concept (author bibliography/discography analog for a filmmaker).

confidence.why: fetch_quality full; page structure, forms, CTAs, embeds and proof all captured clearly; only design tokens (fonts/colors) missing so design_style is inferred.

gap_findings: Personal creative hub has NO single conversion action — it fans out to newsletter, film pre-order (Vimeo VOD), Patreon, PayPal donate, Linktree, and social follows; the 5-engine/one-action model must pick a dominant intent it doesn't truly have.
The ubiquitous 'Share this:' outbound social-sharing widget has no canonical section — followStrip is the nearest but semantically inverted (amplify vs follow).
A dated festival/awards timeline is a recurring creative-professional need with no canonical section id (mapped to results/miscellaneous).
